title: BugHunt: Fix Scheduler Connection EOF Errors in go-service
description: Address recurring EOF errors (230x frequency) when go-service notifies scheduler about finished queries

root_cause_analysis.issue: Scheduler service at 127.0.0.1:9095 closes connections prematurely
root_cause_analysis.symptoms: EOF errors when notifying scheduler, connection failures, lost notifications
root_cause_analysis.impact: Query completion notifications not reaching scheduler, potential workflow stalls

solution_summary: Implement connection pooling with retry logic, health checks, and TCP keep-alive configuration
implementation_components: Connection Pool Manager: Maintain 10 persistent connections with validation
Retry Logic: 3 attempts with exponential backoff (100ms, 200ms, 400ms)
Health Checker: Cache-based health validation with 5s TTL
Connection Configuration: 5s timeout, 10s read/write deadline, 30s keep-alive
files_modified: scheduler_processor.go - Add connection pooling and retry logic
expected_outcomes: Eliminate EOF errors on scheduler notification
Improve reliability of query completion notifications
Provide visibility into scheduler connectivity issues
Enable graceful degradation when scheduler is unavailable

documentation_created.task_1287_code_patch.json: Concrete Go code examples and patches

next_steps: 3. Add unit tests for connection pool
4. Add integration tests for retry logic
